original_user_request: Create a 3D Crossy Road game in a single HTML file using Three.js loaded from CDN. Include lane-based roads, obstacles, player character, scoring, and controls.
precise_rewrite: Develop a 3D Crossy Road game within a single HTML file utilizing Three.js sourced from a CDN. The game should feature lane-based roads, various obstacles, a controllable player character, a scoring system, and responsive controls.
assumptions: The user desires a fully functional game that can be played in any modern web browser without additional installations.

tradeoffs.name: Speed vs Quality
tradeoffs.rationale: Balancing the time required to develop the game against its overall quality and polish.

tradeoffs.name: Realism vs Fun
tradeoffs.rationale: Deciding whether to prioritize a more realistic 3D environment or a more engaging, simplified gameplay experience.

tradeoffs.name: Security vs Cost
tradeoffs.rationale: Evaluating the trade-off between ensuring the game is secure and minimizing development costs.

speed.rationale: The user values a quick turnaround time for the project.

quality.rationale: While speed is important, maintaining high quality ensures a better end product.

realism.rationale: Realism adds depth to the game but may not be as crucial as fun and quality.

fun.rationale: The primary goal is to create an enjoyable game experience for the user.

security.rationale: Security is important but not as critical as speed, quality, and fun.

cost.rationale: Cost should be considered to ensure the project remains feasible within budget constraints.

definition_of_done: The game is fully functional and can be played in any modern web browser.
All required features (lane-based roads, obstacles, player character, scoring, controls) are implemented.
The game adheres to the user's specified requirements and preferences.
conflict_rules: In case of a conflict between speed and quality, prioritize quality as it directly impacts the user experience.
If security is compromised, pause development until necessary measures are taken to ensure safety.
When cost constraints limit functionality, focus on essential features while maintaining a balance with other priorities.
